headline: Ecuador trade deal: Trump administration targets lower food and tech levies
summary: Investing.com — U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer signed a finalized trade agreement with Ecuador on Friday, reducing tariffs across several industrial and agricultural sectors as the Trump administration continues to overhaul its Western Hemisphere trade strategy. The deal, signed alongside Ecuadorian Minister Luis Alberto Jaramillo, opens access to a market of 18 million consumers for U.S. exporters of machinery, IT, and chemicals.
